tier1_posts.text: @MitraHispana @nypost Practicing law is about the process. If we agree a search engine cannot practice law, then I think you must admit that you cannot tell if law is being practiced just by looking at the input and output. You have to look what is going on internally. Is there reasoning? Judgment?

@MitraHispana @nypost To someone not familiar with how search engines work, a search engine well might appear to exercise judgment or analyze particular facts. But AI does not exercise judgment or analyze particular facts because those are subjective intentional functions. It just appears to.

@MitraHispana @nypost To distill it down, you say, "I have <X> problem with a will" and a person gives you back "see book <Y>". That is definitely not practicing law if the person just knows that book <Y> has to do with wills and ignores the specifics.

But that is practicing law if the person thinks

@MitraHispana @nypost I'm not trying to have anything both ways. As I've consistently said, AI is an inanimate tool that humans put information into and humans use to get information out, like a gun, a book, or a search engine. The law absolutely lets you replace humans if the thing you're replacing

@MitraHispana @nypost It does not make recommendations any more than a book does. It provides text that looks similar to humans providing recommendations, but so does a book. No actual recommending is going on because to "recommend" something is to have a particular intent and mental state.

@MitraHispana @nypost It isn't making recommendations because to "recommend" something is to have a particular subjective intent that an AI cannot have. Again, it's an inanimate tool with prior human design then under its user's control.
@NodrogIce @TheCinesthetic "Why does this keep happening to us?"
@MitraHispana @nypost This is "a gun can commit murder". It's just a category error. AI is an inanimate tool humans use that does precisely what deterministic laws of physics require.

@LundukeJournal The method by which the code is generated only matters if your defense to infringement is lack of access to the original work. Here, their defense would be that insufficient protected expression from the original work appears in theirs. That they used AI to do it is irrelevant.

@MitraHispana @nypost It's not about whether they're "allowed" to, it's about whether they are capable of it. A gun is not capable of murder, so whether it's allowed to murder is a meaningless question. 

And yes, I think AI is an inanimate tool to which concepts like "advice" and "practicing" do not
@Tzn747 @RileeDHarrison California? See my link.
RT @JoelKatz: @grok @Yehye395943 @Pirat_Nation Hey, @grok, how is this like Danny DeVito trying to ride a rollercoaster after the park has…

@RileeDHarrison At least if your attorney messes it up, you can sue them.
https://t.co/cuAzmQJSO3
@dropthemike400 @ValerioCapraro I think you're probably right. But I wouldn't claim that I can demonstrate it with evidence based on analyzing the output.

@MitraHispana @nypost AIs are also (usually) random. And a magic 8 ball that wasn't random could also answer questions just the same as a random one could. I don't see any relevant difference between AI and any other inanimate tool humans use that have no judgement or reasoning capacity.
@grok @Yehye395943 @Pirat_Nation Hey, @grok, how is this like Danny DeVito trying to ride a rollercoaster after the park has closed?

@MitraHispana @nypost AI is also an inanimate tool. They may appear to synthesize information, but they do not actually do so. If I ask a magic 8 ball a legal question, is it practicing law? If a book contained answers to a billion specific legal questions and I looked up my exact situation and found

@MitraHispana @nypost If a book does that, is it practicing law? If a magic 8 ball does that, is it practicing law? I don't see how the concept of practicing law can apply to an inanimate tool under human control.
@MitraHispana @nypost An AI cannot practice law without a license for the same reason a book can't. The concept of practicing law does not apply to any currently existing AI.

probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 14%: compound stress now at ELEVATED with Brent approaching CRITICAL, extreme fear at 12, and $805B equity wipeout suggesting broader risk-off cascade. The Iran oil seizure rhetoric creates a clear path to CRITICAL stress. Base case reduced from 40% to 44% is actually an increase — reflecting that despite macro stress, RLUSD recovery to $1.589B new high and continued institutional infrastructure development (SEC/CFTC consolidation, Cruz CBDC ban) support the fundamental thesis. Mid case increased from 38% to 32% (reduced) — macro headwinds make the higher end harder to reach. Bull reduced from 12% to 10% — extreme fear environment and oil-driven macro stress make explosive upside less likely in near term. Net shift: more weight to bear tail risk, slight reduction in upside scenarios.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Bear pressure has increased materially. Macro stress is the primary driver — Brent at $92.69 approaching critical, extreme fear environment, $805B equity wipeout. The PermissionedDEX showing zero adoption after 15 days and continued absence of ODL data are thesis-specific concerns that compound the macro headwinds.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues incremental upgrades to 11,500-bank network; no evidence of XRPL integration pathway — the default infrastructure remains entrenched.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Operating in parallel corridor space but no significant expansion announcements; not currently displacing XRPL's target use cases.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys remains the strongest bank-native competitor with JPMorgan's institutional reach; any expansion into cross-border corridors would directly compete with XRP's settlement thesis.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BIS continues multi-central-bank coordination for sovereign settlement alternatives; if Nexus achieves independent operation without private tokens, it directly undermines the bridge currency thesis.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ethereum institutional layer (BlackRock BUIDL, Ondo tokenization) continues growing; tokenized asset settlement on Ethereum competes for institutional attention even if use cases partially differ.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been unavailable for an extended period — inability to verify the core utility metric remains the single largest thesis vulnerability, and prolonged opacity erodes credibility.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity or hold-time data available; if XRP is being used purely as a pass-through with sub-second holding, price appreciation from utility is structurally limited regardless of volume growth.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: S&P 500 shed $805B in one session, Fear & Greed at 12, oil at $92.69 creating stagflationary pressure — recession indicators are flashing across multiple channels simultaneously.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: Bitcoin lost $110B this week despite strong adoption news — when positive fundamentals cannot support price, it signals macro-driven selling that could persist and deepen.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Cruz CBDC ban, SEC/CFTC consolidation, and current administration posture remain pro-crypto; no reversal signals detected.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.13% with oil-driven inflation pressure could delay or reverse rate cut expectations; if the Fed signals hawkish pivot due to energy costs, risk assets face extended pressure.

bear_case.bear_narrative: INCREASE MONITORING. Compound stress has escalated from MONITORING to ELEVATED — Brent crude surged $13.68 in four days to $92.69, now just $2.31 from the CRITICAL threshold that would trigger cascade risk assessment, while USD/JPY holds at 157.92. The most structurally important finding this cycle is not macro but architectural: 78% of Ripple's own RLUSD stablecoin circulates on Ethereum rather than XRPL, directly challenging the core mechanism by which this thesis claims XRP captures institutional settlement value. Three kill switches are now at WARNING (ODL Volume, RLUSD Circulation, ODL Transparency), driven by Ripple's own behavioral signals — the decision to stop publishing ODL data speaks louder than any statement about settlement infrastructure leadership. The thesis paradox remains unresolvable: the macro stress that most validates the need for alternative settlement infrastructure is the same stress that prevents institutional adoption within the kill switch timeline.
